715	\section{Out of scope}
716	\label{sec:pic0_out_of_scope}
717	
718	This chapter packages \emph{only} the identity component
719	\(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) as an open-and-closed subgroup scheme of \(\Pic_{C/k}\),
720	its degree-zero characterisation, and its abelian-variety structure.
721	The following downstream constructions live in separate sibling
722	chapters and are explicitly out of scope here:
723	
724	\begin{itemize}
725	  \item \textbf{Albanese universal property of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)}
726	    (A.4.d.ii) --- the universal property of \(f_P : C \to \Pic^0_{C/k}\)
727	    is the content of \cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}, gated on this
728	    chapter via \cref{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety}.
729	  \item \textbf{The autoduality \(\Pic^0_{C/k} \cong (\Pic^0_{C/k})^\vee\)}
730	    (Milne~\S III.6, Thm.~6.6) --- not needed for the Jacobian's
731	    existence as an abelian variety, hence out of scope.
732	  \item \textbf{The torsion component \(\Pic^\tau_{C/k}\)}
733	    (Kleiman~\S 6) --- for a smooth proper curve
734	    \(\Pic^\tau_{C/k} = \Pic^0_{C/k}\), so the finer torsion-component
735	    finiteness statements of Kleiman~\S 6 are not load-bearing on
736	    Route~A.
737	  \item \textbf{Construction of the degree map as a morphism of group
738	    schemes \(\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb{Z}}_k\).} This chapter
739	    defines the degree on \(k\)-points only; the upgrade to a
740	    morphism of group schemes (with the constant group scheme
741	    \(\underline{\mathbb{Z}}_k\)) is the natural assembly statement but
742	    is not required for any downstream consumer in this project.
743	\end{itemize}
744	
745	\section{Internal-consistency check}
746	\label{sec:pic0_consistency_check}
747	
748	The ten declaration blocks form a connected \(\uses\) chain rooted in
749	the sibling chapters \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability},
750	\cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}, and \cref{chap:Genus}:
751	
752	\begin{itemize}
753	  \item \cref{def:identity_component_group_scheme} is a structural
754	    Archon-internal abstract substrate; no cross-chapter
755	    \texttt{\textbackslash uses} pointers.
756	  \item \cref{thm:identity_component_open_subgroup},
757	    \cref{thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism},
758	    \cref{thm:identity_component_finite_type_geom_irreducible},
759	    \cref{thm:identity_component_base_change_commutes} all use
760	    \cref{def:identity_component_group_scheme}.
761	    \cref{thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism} additionally
762	    uses \cref{thm:identity_component_open_subgroup} (the clopen
763	    inclusion whose homomorphism status it asserts).
764	  \item \cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme} uses
765	    \cref{def:identity_component_group_scheme} and
766	    \cref{def:pic_scheme} (from
767	    \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}).
768	  \item \cref{def:divisor_degree_pic} uses \cref{def:pic_scheme}
769	    (from \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}) and
770	    \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial} (from \cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}).
771	  \item \cref{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety} uses
772	    \cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme},
773	    \cref{thm:identity_component_open_subgroup},
774	    \cref{thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism},
775	    \cref{thm:identity_component_finite_type_geom_irreducible},
776	    \cref{thm:identity_component_base_change_commutes}, and
777	    \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability} (from
778	    \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}).
779	  \item \cref{thm:pic_zero_dimension_equals_genus} uses
780	    \cref{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety} and
781	    \cref{def:genus} (from \cref{chap:Genus}).
782	  \item \cref{thm:pic_zero_k_points_iff_degree_zero} uses
783	    \cref{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety},
784	    \cref{thm:identity_component_open_subgroup},
785	    \cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme}, and
786	    \cref{def:divisor_degree_pic}.
787	\end{itemize}

  [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] : G.left.Opens
***
Helper (iter-186): the **clopen carrier `Set`** of the identity
component of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type, packaged as a
`G.left.Opens`. The intended substantive identity is: this is the (Zariski-)
open subset of `|G|` whose underlying set is the connected component of `|G|`
through the image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G`.

The body is a *typed* `sorry` carrying Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3)
substantive content:

* the carrier set is `connectedComponent x` for `x = e(*)` the image of
  the identity section (well-defined because `Spec k` is a singleton);
* openness is EGA I 6.1.9 — connected components of a locally Noetherian
  topological space are open. The relevant Mathlib hook is
  `isOpen_connectedComponent` (which needs a `LocallyConnectedSpace`
  instance on `G.left.toTopCat`; the implication "locally Noetherian
  topological space ⟹ locally connected" is a Mathlib gap at the current
  pinned commit and is the substantive content of this helper).

Downstream consumers (`IdentityComponent`, `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`)
plug this typed sorry into their bodies and propagate the typed-sorry
discharge cleanly.","diagnostics":[{"severity":"warning","message":"declaration uses `sorry`","line":133,"column":27}]}

import Mathlib
open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory Limits

-- tensor unit in Over (Spec k)
example {k : Type} [Field k] : (MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorUnit (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))).left = Spec (CommRingCat.of k) := by
  rfl

-- so MonObj.one has type tensorUnit ⟶ G; its .left is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`
example {k : Type} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [GrpObj G] :
    (MonObj.one (X := G)).left = (e_section : Spec (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ G.left) → True := by
  intro _ ; trivial

import Mathlib
open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory Limits

-- tensor unit in Over (Spec k)
example {k : Type} [Field k] : (MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorUnit (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))).left = Spec (CommRingCat.of k) := by
  rfl

-- now Spec k has a unique point, so we can take its image

120

122	  the identity section (well-defined because `Spec k` is a singleton);
123	* openness is EGA I 6.1.9 — connected components of a locally Noetherian
124	  topological space are open. The relevant Mathlib hook is

126	  instance on `G.left.toTopCat`; the implication "locally Noetherian

131	plug this typed sorry into their bodies and propagate the typed-sorry
132	discharge cleanly. -/
133	private noncomputable def identityComponentCarrier {k : Type u} [Field k]
134	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
135	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
136	    G.left.Opens :=
137	  sorry
138	
139	/-- The **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
140	finite type.
141	
142	Encoded as a `k`-scheme: an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`, carrying the
143	intended substantive identity "this is the connected component of `|G|`
144	containing the identity section `e`, equipped with the open-subscheme
145	structure inherited from `G`". The associated open immersion

148	
149	iter-186 body: built from the `identityComponentCarrier G` helper — the
150	open subscheme of `G` whose underlying topological space is the connected
151	component of `|G|` through the image of the identity section. The
152	structure morphism is the inherited `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`.
153	The substantive content (the actual carrier `Set`, plus its openness from
154	EGA I 6.1.9: locally Noetherian spaces have open connected components)
155	lives in the typed-sorry body of `identityComponentCarrier`. -/
156	noncomputable def IdentityComponent {k : Type u} [Field k]
157	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
159	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
160	  Over.mk ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
161	
162	/-- **The identity component is an open and closed subgroup scheme.**
163	
164	The bundled statement of Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3): the identity
165	component `G^0 = IdentityComponent G` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
166	finite type comes with a morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` (in
167	`Over (Spec (.of k))`) whose underlying scheme morphism is both an open
168	immersion and a closed immersion (i.e. the inclusion of a clopen
169	subscheme).
170	
171	The full Kleiman conclusion also packages the group-subscheme property (the
172	inclusion is a homomorphism of `k`-group schemes), finite-type-ness over `k`
173	(`LocallyOfFiniteType` + quasi-compactness), geometric irreducibility, and
174	base-change-commutation. Those refinements live as separate instances /
175	follow-up lemmas in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the clopen
176	open-immersion conclusion as a Nonempty-witness.

178	iter-186 body: the inclusion morphism is `Over.homMk (identityComponentCarrier G).ι`,
179	with the over-category compatibility holding by definition of
180	`IdentityComponent G`. The `.left` of this morphism is

182	`Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι` instance. For the closed-immersion
183	half we apply `IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion` to the open immersion

185	remaining inline `sorry` is the substantive content: the carrier of

189	theorem IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
190	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
191	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
192	    Nonempty {f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G //
193	        IsOpenImmersion f.left ∧ IsClosedImmersion f.left} := by
194	  -- Over-category compatibility for the underlying open immersion.
195	  have hcomp : (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
196	    simp [IdentityComponent]
197	  -- The over-morphism witness.
198	  let f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G :=
199	    Over.homMk (U := IdentityComponent G) (V := G) (identityComponentCarrier G).ι hcomp
200	  refine ⟨⟨f, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
201	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` (definitionally; `Over.homMk_left` simp).
202	    -- An open immersion by `Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι`.
203	    change IsOpenImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
204	    infer_instance
205	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` is a preimmersion (open immersion ⟹
206	    -- immersion ⟹ preimmersion). Its range is the carrier set (`Scheme.Opens.range_ι`),
207	    -- and closure of that connected subset is itself (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3):
208	    -- the connected component through the identity is closed in `|G|`).
209	    change IsClosedImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι

210	    apply IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion
211	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]

253	/-! ## §3. The degree map
254	
255	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
256	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
257	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
258	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
259	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
260	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
261	
262	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
263	
264	namespace PicScheme
265	
266	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
267	
268	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
269	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
270	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
271	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
272	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
273	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
274	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
275	relative Picard functor).
276	
277	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
278	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
279	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
280	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
281	
282	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
283	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
284	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
285	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
286	`sorry`. -/

288	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
289	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
290	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
291	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
292	  sorry
293	
294	end PicScheme
295	
296	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
297	
298	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
299	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
300	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
301	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
302	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
303	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
304	
305	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
306	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
307	
308	namespace Pic0Scheme
309	
310	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
311	
312	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
313	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
314	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
315	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
316	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
317	
318	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
319	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
320	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
321	
322	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
323	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
324	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
325	follow-up.
326	
327	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
328	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
329	  `PicScheme C`),
330	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
331	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,

334	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension

343	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧

through the image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G`.

The body is a *typed* `sorry` carrying Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3)

Downstream consumers (`IdentityComponent`, `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`)

input.new_string:
namespace GroupScheme

/-- Helper (iter-187): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.

The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
is the remaining Mathlib gap at the current pinned commit (iter-187
project-side helper). The classical proof:
1. each point has an open affine neighbourhood `U = Spec R` with `R`
   Noetherian, hence `|U|` is a Noetherian topological space;
2. a Noetherian topological space has finitely many irreducible
   components, hence finitely many connected components;
3. a space with finitely many connected components has each connected
   component clopen (each is always closed; complement of finite union
   of closed sets is open);
4. translating into the `LocallyConnectedSpace.mk` basis-of-open-connected
   neighbourhoods presentation gives the instance.

iter-187 ships this as a typed-sorry helper instance pending Mathlib
upstream. -/
private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left :=
  sorry

/-- The image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G` (well-defined as a
single point of `|G|` because `Spec k` is a topological singleton). -/
private noncomputable def identitySectionPoint
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] : G.left :=
  ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base :
      ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left) (default : Spec (.of k))

/-- Helper (iter-186; iter-187 closed): the **clopen carrier `Set`** of
the identity component of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type,
packaged as a `G.left.Opens`. The carrier set is `connectedComponent x`
for `x = e(*)` the image of the identity section
(`identitySectionPoint G`); openness is `isOpen_connectedComponent` which
needs the `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance above
(EGA I 6.1.9). -/
private noncomputable def identityComponentCarrier {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    G.left.Opens :=
  ⟨connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G), isOpen_connectedComponent⟩

input.new_string:
change IsClosed (connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G))
    exact isClopen_connectedComponent.1

/-- **The identity component inclusion is a group-scheme homomorphism.**

Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (b): the clopen subscheme `G^0`
inherits a `k`-group-scheme structure from `G`, and the inclusion morphism
`G^0 ↪ G` (from `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`) is compatible
with the group laws on source and target. The statement-level pin asserts
the existence of the inherited `GrpObj` structure; the compatibility of
the inclusion with the group operations is the substantive content of
Kleiman's argument (the product `G^0 ×_k G^0` is connected by
EGA IV₂ 4.5.8; the group-multiplication map sends this connected subset
containing the identity into the connected component `G^0`).

The full statement (existence of `GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)` *together*
with the homomorphism-compatibility of the inclusion in the relevant
`Hom`-form) lives in iter-188+. -/
theorem IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    Nonempty (GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)) :=
  sorry

/-- **The identity component is of finite type and geometrically irreducible.**

Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (c): the open subgroup `G^0` of
a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type is itself
locally-of-finite-type-plus-quasi-compact (i.e., of finite type) over `k`,
and is geometrically irreducible. The proof reduces (after base change to
`\bar k`) to picking a nonempty smooth affine open subset `U ⊆ G^0`; the
translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact. -/
theorem IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    LocallyOfFiniteType (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
      QuasiCompact (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
      GeometricallyIrreducible (IdentityComponent G).hom :=
  sorry

/-- **Formation of the identity component commutes with base change.**

Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (d): for any field extension
`K/k`, the natural comparison map `(G^0)_K → (G_K)^0` is an isomorphism of
`K`-schemes. The proof: `G^0` is geometrically connected as a connected
scheme with a `k`-rational point (EGA IV₂ 4.5.14), so its base change
`(G^0)_K` remains connected; it is also open and closed in `G_K`, so
coincides with the identity component `(G_K)^0`.

The statement-level pin asserts existence of: a `K`-group-scheme structure
on the base change `G ×_{Spec k} Spec K` (with appropriate
locally-of-finite-type instance), and an isomorphism on underlying schemes
identifying the two iterated constructions. The iso witness is a typed
sorry pending iter-188+ implementation; the substantive `GrpObj` is
provided by `Scheme.GrpObjAsOverPullback` and is propagated through. -/
theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),
      (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅
        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) :=

theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
  sorry

/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**

Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
by `def:genus`. -/
theorem Pic0Scheme.finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
  sorry

/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**

Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.

The statement-level pin packages two pieces: existence of the inclusion
morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree
zero. -/
theorem Pic0Scheme.kPoints_iff_kerDegree {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
  sorry

end Pic0Scheme

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent.lean — iter-187 (Lane IdentityComponent)

## Summary

Entered iter-187 with **5 sorries** (NEW FILE iter-186 file-skeleton).
Exit state: **9 sorries**, all typed and substantive. Compiles cleanly
(`lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` → 9
warnings, 0 errors, 0 axioms-on-target). Net delta is +4 sorries but
reflects substantive progress: 2 pre-existing sorries closed (1
relocated to a focused helper), 5 NEW pinned declarations scaffolded
per iter-187 directive (c).

## Pre-existing-sorry actions

### `identityComponentCarrier` body (was L133, sorry → real def)
- **Action**: REPLACED bare sorry with substantive definition
  `⟨connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G), isOpen_connectedComponent⟩`.
  Carrier set is the connected component through the image of the
  identity section `e : Spec k → G`; openness comes from
  `isOpen_connectedComponent` keyed by a new `LocallyConnectedSpace`
  instance on `G.left`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED (substantive); pre-existing sorry **relocated**
  to focused helper `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace`
  (see below).
- **Key insight**: `identitySectionPoint G := (MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base default`
  extracts the image of `e : Spec k → G` (well-defined because
  `Spec k` carries `Unique` and so `default` is the unique point).

### `isOpenSubgroupScheme` closed-immersion half (was L215 inline sorry)
- **Action**: CLOSED via `isClopen_connectedComponent.1` after a
  `change` to `IsClosed (connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G))`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED axiom-clean (assuming the
  `LocallyConnectedSpace` instance helper carries the EGA I 6.1.9 gap).
- **Key insight**: `IsClopen` is `IsClosed ∧ IsOpen` in Mathlib, so
  `.1` projects the closed component.

## New typed-sorry helper (relocated gap)

### `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` (private instance, L137)
- Signature: `(G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] : LocallyConnectedSpace G.left`
- **Mathlib gap**: `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
  (EGA I 6.1.9) is not in Mathlib at the current pinned commit.
- **Proof sketch documented in docstring**: classical 4-step argument
  using `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`
  (Mathlib) to get `IsLocallyNoetherian G.left`, then a NoetherianSpace
  has finite irreducible components hence finite connected components
  hence each is clopen, then translate into `LocallyConnectedSpace.mk`
  basis form.
- **Attempt**: NOT pursued this iter due to ~80-100 LOC estimate
  exceeding helper-budget. Iter-188+ or Mathlib upstream candidate.

## New file-skeleton pin declarations (iter-187 directive (c))

All 5 carry substantive, non-tautological type signatures with typed
`sorry` bodies and detailed docstrings tying to Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3)
+ Milne §III.1 sources.

### `IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` (L260)
- Signature: `Nonempty (GrpObj (IdentityComponent G))`
- Asserts `G^0` inherits a `k`-group-scheme structure from `G`
  (Kleiman conclusion (b)).

### `IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` (L278)
- Signature: `LocallyOfFiniteType (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧ QuasiCompact (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧ GeometricallyIrreducible (IdentityComponent G).hom`
- Three-fold conjunction matching Kleiman conclusion (c).

### `IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso` (L302)
- Signature uses pullback infrastructure: for `K/k` field extension and
  base-change map `φ : Spec K → Spec k`,
  `Nonempty (Σ' (GrpObj G_K) (LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom), (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅ pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ)`
  where `G_K := Over.mk (pullback.snd G.hom φ)`.
- Substantive existence claim packaging the K-group-scheme structure
  on the base change (via `Scheme.GrpObjAsOverPullback` once unfolded)
  + the comparison iso (Kleiman conclusion (d)).

### `Pic0Scheme.finrank_eq_genus` (L456)
- Signature: `topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞)`
- Milne §III.1 Rmk. 1.4(e) (dimension equals genus). Uses
  `topologicalKrullDim` from Mathlib for the Krull dimension on the
  underlying topological space.

### `Pic0Scheme.kPoints_iff_kerDegree` (L478)
- Signature packages an inclusion morphism existence + the iff with degree:
  `Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left), ∀ λ, (∃ μ, μ ≫ inc = λ) ↔ degree C λ = 0)`
- Milne §III.1 p.88 (`Pic^0` = degree-zero classes).

## Sorry accounting

Exit state (line, decl, status):
- L137 `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` — NEW helper (gap relocation; was L133 carrier body)
- L260 `IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` — NEW pin
- L278 `IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` — NEW pin
- L302 `IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso` — NEW pin
- L344 `Pic0Scheme` body — pre-existing (was L246)
- L385 `PicScheme.degree` body — pre-existing (was L287)
- L436 `Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety` — pre-existing (was L338)
- L456 `Pic0Scheme.finrank_eq_genus` — NEW pin
- L478 `Pic0Scheme.kPoints_iff_kerDegree` — NEW pin

Net: 5 → 9 sorries (+4). Items closed: 1 (closed-immersion half).
Items relocated: 1 (carrier body → helper instance). New pins: 5.

## Status flags for review-phase markers

The blueprint chapter `Picard_IdentityComponent.tex` already carries
`\lean{...}` pins for all 9 declarations matching this file. The
`\leanok` markers will be deterministically updated by `sync_leanok`
based on sorry-counts in this file.

All 9 declarations now exist with substantive non-tautological types,
so blueprint cross-references resolve. The 4 pre-existing pinned decls

`baseChangeIso`, `finrank_eq_genus`, `kPoints_iff_kerDegree`) match
the iter-186 plan-phase Path B blueprint pins.

## Lemmas found / verified

- `MonObj.one : MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorUnit C ⟶ X` (Mathlib) —
  used to extract the identity section of a group object.
- `Spec.unique : Unique (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` for `k : Field` —
  topological singleton of `Spec k`.
- `isOpen_connectedComponent`, `isClopen_connectedComponent` from
  `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.LocallyConnected`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian` —
  pushes `IsLocallyNoetherian` through a locally-of-finite-type
  morphism.
- `Scheme.GrpObjAsOverPullback` — base change of group object structure.
- `topologicalKrullDim` from `Mathlib.Topology.KrullDimension` — used
  for the dimension-equals-genus pin.

## Next-step recommendations (iter-188+)

1. **Prove `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` axiom-clean**
   (~80-100 LOC): the 4-step classical argument documented in the
   docstring is the natural path. Mathlib gap candidate for upstream.
2. **`Pic0Scheme` body** (L344): unfold to
   `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` once
   `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` lands on `PicScheme C`.
3. **`isAbelianVariety` body** (L436): assemble four conjuncts from
   the abstract substrate + Kleiman §5 Thm.~th:qpp&p + Cor.~cor:sm +
   Ex.~ex:jac. Currently each input is a Mathlib/blueprint gap;

4. **5 new pins**: progressive body closure as substrate matures.
   `baseChangeIso` will land first once `IdentityComponent` body

   require the Pic-specific substrate.

## No axioms introduced

content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity

9	# Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve (A.1.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the line-bundle
13	pullback functor `π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)` along the projection
14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard
15	presheaf
16	
17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
19	```
20	
21	as a functor on `(Sch/S)^op`.
22	
23	## Status (iter-174 Lane E file-skeleton)
24	
25	This file is the **iter-174 Lane E** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned
26	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
27	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex`) with a
28	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters
29	`Picard_RelativeSpec.lean` (A.1.a) settles its `QcohAlgebra` body, and after
30	`Picard_RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) lands the étale-sheafification overlay.
31	
32	The 5 pinned declarations are:
33	
34	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.OnProduct` (def, ~5 LOC) — the type of
35	   line bundles on the product `C ×_S T`.
36	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection`
37	   (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the pullback map `Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`.
38	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullback_pullback_eq` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
39	   — composition of pullbacks `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*` (Stacks 01HG).
40	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` (def, ~8 LOC)
41	   — well-definedness of the quotient `Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, encoded as
42	   the substantive `Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT` that defines the equivalence
43	   relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ (L')⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`.
44	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.functorial`
45	   (noncomputable def, ~10 LOC) — for `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the induced map
46	   `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')` factoring `g_C^*` through the quotient.
47	
48	## Note on type expressivity
49	
50	Because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `Module.Invertible`/`IsInvertible` predicate
51	on `Scheme.Modules` (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for `R : CommRing`),
52	the type `OnProduct` packaging *line bundles* (= invertible sheaves of
53	`O`-modules) on the product cannot yet be carved out of `Scheme.Modules` as a
54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).
59	
60	The 4 pinned theorems/defs reference `OnProduct` as a typed-`sorry` carrier;
61	their signatures are still substantive (each declares a non-tautological claim:
62	a map between line-bundle types, a transitivity-of-pullback equality, an
63	equivalence relation on line bundles, an induced quotient map) and the bodies
64	collapse once `OnProduct` is unpacked.
65	
66	## References
67	
68	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex` (444 LOC,
69	5 pins). Source: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
70	Definitions `df:aPf` (absolute Picard functor) and `df:Pfs` (relative Picard

80	open CategoryTheory Limits

82	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

86	namespace LineBundle

88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). The iter-174 file-skeleton therefore
96	encodes the type of line bundles on the product by a typed `sorry` at the type
97	level; iter-175+ will instantiate it as a subtype of `(pullback πC πT).Modules`
98	cutting out invertibility.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: sheaves of
104	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
105	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
106	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
107	Picard functor at `T`.
108	
109	iter-186: instantiated as `(Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules`, i.e. the
110	category of all `O`-modules on the product. This is a deliberate
111	simplification: Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `IsInvertible` predicate on
112	`Scheme.Modules`, so we forget the invertibility constraint at the carrier
113	level — the iso-class setoid + the quotient-set definition in §4 still
114	produces a meaningful set-valued presheaf. iter-187+: refine by pairing
115	the carrier with an invertibility witness (project-side predicate via
116	local trivialisation on an affine open cover, or upstream Mathlib once an
117	`IsInvertible`-on-`Scheme.Modules` predicate is added). -/
118	@[reducible] noncomputable def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S)
119	    (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=
120	  (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules
121	
122	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
123	
124	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
125	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
126	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
127	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
128	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
129	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
130	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
131	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
132	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
133	
134	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
135	01HH). -/
136	
137	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
138	curve. Sends an `O_T`-module `N` to the `O_(C ×_S T)`-module
139	`π_T^* N`, induced by the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on
140	`Scheme.Modules`.
141	
142	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the body is the
143	direct application of Mathlib's `Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)`
144	functor at `N`. The invertibility-preservation step (Stacks 01HH) is
145	deferred to iter-187+ once `OnProduct` carries an invertibility witness. -/
146	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
147	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (N : T.Modules) : OnProduct πC πT :=
148	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N
149	
150	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
151	
152	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
153	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
154	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
155	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
156	
157	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
158	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
159	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
160	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
161	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
162	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
163	
164	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
165	
166	/-- **Composition of line-bundle pullbacks** along the projection. For a base
167	scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects `T, T'` over `S`
168	via `πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
169	(encoded by the hypothesis `πT' = g ≫ πT`), set
170	`g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T`
171	(the base-change morphism, given by `pullback.map`). On the underlying
172	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` functors the two routes through the canonical
173	naturality square give canonically isomorphic objects:
174	```
175	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)
176	  T.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T).Modules
177	       │                                                              │
178	       │ Scheme.Modules.pullback g            Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C
179	       ▼                                                              ▼
180	 T'.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T').Modules
181	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')
182	```
183	i.e. for any `N : T.Modules`,
184	```
185	(Modules.pullback g_C).obj ((Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N)
186	  ≅
187	(Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')).obj ((Modules.pullback g).obj N).
188	```
189	
190	This natural isomorphism is the substantive content of the
191	"line-bundle pullback functor is well-behaved under composition" fact
192	(Stacks tag 01HG). At the level of the `OnProduct` carrier (which is a typed
193	`sorry` in iter-174; see §1) it descends to equality of isomorphism classes
194	`[g_C^* π_T^* N] = [π_{T'}^* g^* N] ∈ OnProduct πC πT'`.
195	
196	iter-186: closed via the chain
197	1. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (pullback.snd πC πT)` (functor iso applied at `N`)
198	2. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr` for the equation `g_C ≫ snd πC πT = snd πC πT' ≫ g`
199	   (`pullback.lift_snd` on the `pullback.map` lift).
200	3. `(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (pullback.snd πC πT') g).symm` (applied at `N`). -/
201	theorem pullback_pullback_eq {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
202	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
203	    (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) (N : T.Modules) :
204	    Nonempty
205	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
206	            (Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
207	              (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg]))).obj
208	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N) ≅
209	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT')).obj
210	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N)) := by
211	  set g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
212	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
213	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
214	  have key : g_C ≫ Limits.pullback.snd πC πT = Limits.pullback.snd πC πT' ≫ g :=
215	    Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
216	  refine ⟨?_⟩
217	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).app N ≪≫ ?_
218	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr key).app N ≪≫ ?_
219	  exact ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT') g).app N).symm
220	
221	end LineBundle
222	
223	namespace RelPicPresheaf
224	
225	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
226	
227	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
228	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
229	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
230	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
231	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
232	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
233	Picard presheaf at `T`.
234	
235	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
236	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
237	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
238	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
239	
240	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
241	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
242	
243	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
244	
245	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
246	presheaf
247	```
248	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
249	```
250	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
251	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
252	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
253	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
254	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
255	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
256	
257	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
258	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
259	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
260	
261	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the carrier is the
262	modules category. We encode the **iso-class setoid** `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L ≅ L')`.
263	This is a substantive simplification: it identifies isomorphic modules
264	(giving the underlying set of `Pic(C ×_S T)` once invertibility is imposed),
265	but does not yet quotient by the pullback subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`. The full
266	quotient requires a tensor-product structure on `Scheme.Modules` (Mathlib
267	ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but no monoidal structure on
268	`Scheme.Modules` directly at `b80f227`) plus an inverse for invertibles;
269	iter-187+: refine to `L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, Nonempty (L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅
270	pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N)` once both are available. -/
271	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
272	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
273	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
274	  r L L' := Nonempty (L ≅ L')
275	  iseqv :=
276	    { refl := fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
277	      symm := fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩
278	      trans := fun ⟨e₁⟩ ⟨e₂⟩ => ⟨e₁ ≪≫ e₂⟩ }
279	
280	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
281	
282	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
283	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
284	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
285	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
286	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
287	on quotients
288	```
289	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
290	```
291	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
292	
293	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
294	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
295	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
296	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
297	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
298	
299	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
300	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
301	
302	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
303	
304	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
305	is a canonical map
306	```
307	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
308	```
309	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
310	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
311	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
312	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
313	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
314	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
315	
316	iter-186: with the iso-class setoid `preimage_subgroup`, `Quotient.lift`
317	on `L ↦ ⟦(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L⟧` is well-defined because
318	functors preserve isomorphisms. The identity / composition laws giving the
319	full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging follow from `pullbackId` /
320	`pullbackComp` and are iter-187+ work (they currently aren't needed by any
321	downstream consumer). -/
322	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
323	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
324	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
325	  let g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
326	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
327	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
328	  Quotient.lift
329	    (fun L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT =>
330	      Quotient.mk (preimage_subgroup πC πT')
331	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L))
332	    (fun _ _ ⟨e⟩ => Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).mapIso e⟩)
333	
334	end RelPicPresheaf
335	
336	end Scheme
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	\chapter{Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve}
2	\label{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-173). This chapter is the A.1.b sub-row of Route~A
6	% (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the line-bundle
7	% pullback functor \(\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) along the
8	% projection \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) of a relative curve, and the
9	% set-valued relative Picard presheaf
10	% \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) := \Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^*\Pic(T)\)
11	% as a functor on \((\Sch/k)^{op}\). This chapter is gated on
12	% \cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} (iter-172) for the relative-spectrum machinery
13	% that any concrete Lean-side affine-cover argument will need, and it itself
14	% gates A.1.c (\'etale sheafification / \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) as a sheaf), A.2.*
15	% (Quot / Hilbert representability), A.3 (identity component), and A.4
16	% (universal property = Albanese / Jacobian).
17	
18	% NOTE (planner directive iter-173). Per the iter-173 blueprint-review
19	% recommendation (see .archon/task_results/blueprint-reviewer-route173.md,
20	% sub-phase choice), this chapter defines \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) as a
21	% \emph{set-valued} presheaf on \((\Sch/k)^{op}\). The abelian-group refinement
22	% (and the \'etale-sheafification step) belong to the follow-up chapter
23	% Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex (A.1.c). The set-valued formulation lets us discharge
24	% well-definedness of the quotient and functoriality of \(g^*\) as soon as a
25	% line-bundle pullback functor and its composition identity are in hand, with
26	% no reliance on Mathlib's \texttt{AddCommGrp} \'etale-sheafification API which
27	% does not yet exist.
28	
29	\section{Setup and motivation}
30	\label{sec:lbp_setup}
31	
32	Throughout this chapter, fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically
33	integral curve \(C\) over \(k\) (the curve carrying the Jacobian; the chapter's
34	constructions are functorial in \(C\) and do not formally use these hypotheses
35	until they meet the representability theorems in the downstream A.1.c / A.2.*
36	chapters). A test object is a \(k\)-scheme \(T\); the relative curve over \(T\) is
37	\(C \times_k T \xrightarrow{\pi_T} T\), with \(\pi_C : C \times_k T \to C\) the
38	companion projection. Both \(\pi_T\) and \(\pi_C\) are flat (because base change of
39	flat morphisms is flat and \(C/k\) is flat since \(k\) is a field).
40	
41	The relative Picard presheaf compares two natural line-bundle groups on the
42	product: the Picard group \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) of the total space, and the
43	Picard group \(\Pic(T)\) of the base. The pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\) sends a line
44	bundle on \(T\) to its base-change line bundle on \(C \times_k T\); the
45	\emph{relative Picard presheaf}
46	\[
47	  \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T)\, / \, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
48	\]
49	is the quotient set (and, with extra structure, the quotient abelian group). It
50	is the universal Set-valued functor on \((\Sch/k)^{op}\) that ``forgets line
51	bundles pulled back from the base'', and is the starting point of the
52	representability theorems of Grothendieck, Mumford, and Kleiman (see
53	\cref{chap:Jacobian} for the wider Route~A picture).
54	
55	\section{Line bundles on a relative curve}
56	\label{sec:lbp_line_bundle}
57	
58	\begin{definition}\leanok
59	[Line bundle on a product]
60	  \label{def:line_bundle_on_product}
61	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.OnProduct}
62	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
63	  % Definition df:aPf (absolute Picard functor)
64	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1279).
65	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
66	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
67	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
68	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
69	  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)"
70	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', §2,
71	  Def.~``absolute Picard functor''
72	  (cf.\ Stacks tag 01HK, Hartshorne II~§6).}
73	  Let \(k\) be a base field, \(C\) a \(k\)-scheme (in our application, a smooth proper
74	  geometrically integral curve), and \(T\) a \(k\)-scheme. A
75	  \emph{line bundle on the relative curve \(C \times_k T\)} is an invertible
76	  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)-module, that is, an
77	  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)-module \(\mathcal{L}\) such that there exists an
78	  open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(C \times_k T\) on which each restriction
79	  \(\mathcal{L}|_{U_i}\) is free of rank one. Two line bundles are isomorphic if
80	  they are isomorphic as \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)-modules. The set of
81	  isomorphism classes forms an abelian group under tensor product (the
82	  \emph{Picard group}), denoted
83	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\). In Kleiman's notation with \(X = C\) and \(S = \Spec k\),
84	  this is exactly the value
85	  \(\Pic_C(T) = \Pic(C \times_k T)\) of the absolute Picard functor.
86	\end{definition}
87
