585	\section{Existence of a non-constant rational function}
586	
587	\begin{corollary}
588	
589	
590	\leanok
591	[A genus-\(0\) curve admits a non-constant rational function with a
592	single simple pole]
593	  \label{cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero}
594	  % Alias label consumed under the shorter name by
595	  % \texttt{RiemannRoch\_RationalCurveIso.tex} (RR.4):
596	  \label{cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero}
597	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists_nonconstant_genusZero}
598	  
599	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Exercise~1.1 (the existence of a
600	  % nonconstant rational function regular everywhere except at one
601	  % point on a genus-\(0\) curve, as a direct consequence of the
602	  % Riemann--Roch dimension formula) (read from
603	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314).
604	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "1.1. Let \(X\) be a curve, and let \(P \in X\) be a
605	  % point. Then there exists a nonconstant rational function $f \in
606	  % K(X)\(, which is regular everywhere except at \)P$."
607	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Exercise~1.1, p.~297.}
608	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
609	  \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then
610	  there exists a non-constant rational function \(f \in K(C)\) such that:
611	  \begin{itemize}
612	    \item \(f\) is regular on \(C \setminus \{P\}\), i.e.\ \(\ord_Q(f) \geq 0\)
613	      for every closed point \(Q \in C\) with \(Q \neq P\);
614	    \item \(f\) has at most a simple pole at \(P\), i.e.\
615	      \(\ord_P(f) \geq -1\);
616	    \item \(f \notin \bar k\), equivalently
617	      \(\div(f) \neq 0 \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\).
618	  \end{itemize}
619	  In particular, the pair \((1, f)\) spans
620	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) as a \(\bar k\)-vector space.
621	\end{corollary}
622	
623	\begin{proof}
624	  
625	  By
626	  REF,
627	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\). The constant subspace
628	  \(\bar k \cdot 1 \subset H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\), obtained from the
629	  structural inclusion $\bar k \cong H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
630	  \hookrightarrow H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))$ (the rational function
631	  \(f = 1\), with \(\div(1) + [P] = [P] \geq 0\), under the
632	  identification of
633	  REF), is a one-dimensional
634	  \(\bar k\)-vector subspace. Since the ambient space has dimension \(2\),
635	  the quotient \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) / \bar k \cdot 1\) is a
636	  non-zero one-dimensional \(\bar k\)-vector space; any lift to
637	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) of a non-zero element of the quotient is
638	  an element of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \setminus \bar k \cdot 1\).
639	
640	  Choose such a lift $s \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \setminus \bar k
641	  \cdot 1$. Under the identification of
642	  REF the section
643	  \(s\) corresponds to a rational function \(f \in K(C)^{\times}\)
644	  satisfying \(\div(f) + [P] \geq 0\), equivalently \(\ord_Q(f) \geq 0\)
645	  for every \(Q \neq P\) and \(\ord_P(f) \geq -1\). The choice
646	  \(s \notin \bar k \cdot 1\) means \(f\) is not the image of a constant,
647	  so \(f \notin \bar k\) (every non-zero element of
648	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) \cong \bar k\) corresponds, under the
649	  identification, to a constant element of \(K(C)\)). The three bullets
650	  of the statement are now immediate.
651	
652	  Spanning. Linear independence of \(1\) and \(f\) over \(\bar k\) in
653	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) is the same condition as
654	  \(s \notin \bar k \cdot 1\) above; combined with the dimension count
655	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\), the pair \((1, f)\) is a
656	  \(\bar k\)-basis of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\).
657	
658	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean signature
659	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists\_nonconstant\_genusZero}
660	  returns an element \(f\) of the project's function-field carrier
661	  \(K(C)\) together with proofs of regularity on \(C \setminus \{P\}\) and
662	  the simple-pole bound at \(P\), packaged in the same shape as
663	  REF's output. The
664	  non-constancy is recorded as a separate field; the spanning
665	  statement is downstream and is consumed by \texttt{RR.4} via
666	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections}.
667	\end{proof}
668	
669	\section{Mathlib-status flag: the H\(^1\)-vanishing input}
670	
671	The vanishing \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 0\) on a genus-\(0\) curve
672	(REF) is the
673	critical cohomological input of this chapter. Its closure depends on
674	two Mathlib-status facts, both of which the project already consumes
675	elsewhere:
676	
677	\begin{itemize}
678	  \item \textbf{Long exact sequence of \(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured sheaf
679	    cohomology.} Mathlib (\texttt{b80f227}) ships
680	    \texttt{CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence\_exact} for
681	    \(\Ext^*\)-long-exact-sequences at the abelian-group level. The
682	    project's \(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured pipeline
683	    (REF, \texttt{Cohomology\_StructureSheafModuleK.tex})
684	    inherits it by forget-functor naturality. This bridge is already
685	    consumed by REF of
686	    \texttt{RR.2}, so closing it here introduces no new Mathlib
687	    obligation.
688	  \item \textbf{Vanishing of higher cohomology of a skyscraper sheaf}
689	    on a Noetherian scheme. Hartshorne~III.2.5 gives the flasque
690	    formulation; the project's affine-cover Čech route
691	    (REF and surrounding
692	    \texttt{Cohomology\_StructureSheafModuleK.tex} machinery) computes
693	    the same vanishing for the skyscraper at a closed point by direct
694	    Čech-degeneration. \texttt{RR.2}'s inductive step already invokes
695	    \(\chi(k(P)) = 1\), which is the combined statement
696	    \(\dim H^0(C, k(P)) = 1\) and \(\dim H^i(C, k(P)) = 0\) for \(i \geq 1\),
697	    so the vanishing \(H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\) is already a consumer
698	    obligation of \texttt{RR.2}.
699	\end{itemize}
700	
701	Consequently the H\(^1\)-vanishing
702	REF introduces no
703	Mathlib obligation that is not already on \texttt{RR.2}'s critical
704	path. The genus-\(0\) specialisation should therefore close at the
705	formalisation stage without splitting this chapter or pushing the
706	vanishing back into \texttt{RR.2}.
707	
708	% NOTE (iter-174 plan-agent flag). The general-\(g\) vanishing statement
709	% ``\(H^1(C, \mathcal L) = 0\) whenever \(\deg \mathcal L > 2g - 2\)''
710	% (Hartshorne~IV.1, the Riemann--Roch consequence read covariantly) is
711	% \emph{not} a deliverable of this chapter and is not required for the
712	% genus-\(0\) critical path: the genus-\(0\) argument closes via the long
713	% exact sequence at \(D = 0\) without ever needing the general bound. The
714	% general bound is a Serre-duality-flavoured statement and is queued for
715	% the post-RR-bridge follow-up chapter together with the dualizing sheaf
716	% \(\omega_C\) and the canonical-divisor identification \(K_C \sim -2[P]\)
717	% on \(\mathbb P^1\) (Hartshorne~II.8.20.1).
718	
719	\section{Out of scope}
720	
721	The following items are deliberately \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.3}
722	and are sequenced into the sibling sub-build chapters or queued for
723	follow-up iterations:
724	
725	\begin{itemize}
726	  \item The construction of the invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D)\)
727	    associated to an arbitrary Weil divisor $D = \sum n_i [P_i] \in
728	    \mathrm{Div}(C)$, and the linear-equivalence isomorphism
729	    \(\mathcal O_C(D) \cong \mathcal O_C(D')\) when \(D \sim D'\), are
730	    \emph{not} performed in this chapter. The chapter constructs only
731	    the specialisation \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) at \(D = [P]\) a prime divisor,
732	    which is the only case consumed by the genus-\(0\) classification
733	    chain. The general \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) is queued for the
734	    post-RR-bridge follow-up (where it pairs with the general-\(g\)
735	    Riemann--Roch formula and the canonical-divisor identification).
736	  \item The general-\(g\) statement
737	    $\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = \deg([P]) + 1 -
738	    g(C) + \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P))$, while a direct
739	    consequence of
740	    REF of \texttt{RR.2},
741	    is not separately formalised here; the chapter restricts to the
742	    genus-\(0\) specialisation \(g = 0\) on which
743	    REF is
744	    available.
745	  \item \textbf{Serre duality} $H^1(C, \mathcal L) \cong
746	    H^0(C, \omega_C \otimes \mathcal L^{-1})^*$ and the associated
747	    canonical divisor \(K_C = c_1(\omega_C)\) are \emph{not} used at any
748	    step of this chapter and are not deliverables; the chapter routes
749	    cohomological vanishing through the long exact sequence at
750	    \(D = 0\) (REF)
751	    instead. Serre duality is queued for the post-RR-bridge follow-up.
752	  \item The morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) produced by applying
753	    \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} to the basis \((1, f)\) of
754	    REF,
755	    and its identification as an isomorphism, are the content of
756	    \texttt{RR.4} (sibling chapter
757	    \texttt{RiemannRoch\_RationalIsoP1.tex}, to be added). The present
758	    chapter supplies the basis \((1, f)\) and stops.
759	  \item The general-\(g\) vanishing
760	    \(H^1(C, \mathcal L) = 0\) for \(\deg \mathcal L > 2g - 2\) is
761	    \emph{not} part of this chapter and is not required for the
762	    genus-\(0\) critical path. It is a Serre-duality-flavoured statement
763	    queued for the post-RR-bridge follow-up.
764	  \item The interpretation of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) as the space
765	    of sections of a complete linear system \(|[P]|\) in
766	    projective-space form (Hartshorne~II.7.7) is gestured at by
767	    REF but the
768	    projective-space structure on the underlying set is not separately
769	    formalised; the chapter consumes only the \(\bar k\)-vector space
770	    structure on \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\).
771	\end{itemize}

233	      (CotangentSpace (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})) + 1 =
234	    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) := by
235	  -- Build the κ-linear short exact sequence
236	  --   0 → κ · x̄ → 𝔪/𝔪² → 𝔪̄/𝔪̄² → 0
237	  -- where κ = R/𝔪 (cotangent residue field) and apply
238	  -- Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank
239	  sorry
240	```

8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: finite-length carriers and producer instances
10	
11	Sub-file (3/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	hosts the downstream finite-length carriers (`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`,
13	`IsHModuleHomFinite`), their algebraic bridges (the `HModule` / `HModule'`
14	abbrevs, the H⁰ `LinearEquiv` transport companions, the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction
15	upgrade), and the curve-side producer instances. Upstream sub-files
16	`Presheaf.lean` and `SheafProperty.lean` provide the structure-sheaf object the
17	carriers depend on.
18	
19	Downstream finite-length carriers in this file:
20	
21	* H>0 affine cohomology vanishing is carried by iter-040's
22	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` (the producer instance for the curve case
23	  lives at iter-051).
24	* H⁰ Hom-finiteness is carried by iter-043's **wholespace**
25	  `IsHModuleHomFinite`; the producer instance on a proper geometrically
26	  integral $k$-curve is supplied at iter-046 from Stein finiteness
27	  (`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper`).
28	* The iter-041 per-affine-open variant `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` was an
29	  abandoned attempt (dead scaffolding — $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is not finite
30	  over $k$ on a proper affine open of a non-trivial curve) and has been
31	  removed from this file.
32	
33	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
34	-/

44	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
45	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
46	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
47	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
48	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
49	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
50	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
51	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev HModule
53	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
54	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
55	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
56	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
57	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
58	
59	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
60	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
61	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
62	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any
63	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
64	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
65	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
66	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
67	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
68	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a
69	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
70	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
71	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
72	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
73	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
74	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
75	    HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k]
76	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :=
77	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
78	
79	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
80	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
81	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
82	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
83	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
84	
85	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
86	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
87	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
88	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
89	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` — under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
90	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
91	
92	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
93	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
94	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
95	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
96	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
97	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
98	iter-015+. -/
99	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
100	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
101	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
102	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
103	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
104	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
105	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
106	
107	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
108	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
109	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
110	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
111	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`
112	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
113	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
114	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
115	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
116	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
117	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
118	derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
119	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
120	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
121	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
122	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
123	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
124	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
125	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
126	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
127	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
128	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) :=
129	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
130	
131	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
132	H⁰ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
133	(ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
134	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
135	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the
136	Hom group on the RHS, but we want the Hom hypothesis on the LHS and the
137	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
138	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
139	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
140	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
141	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/
142	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero
143	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
144	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
145	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
146	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
147	    [Module.Finite k ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)] :
148	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
149	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm

115	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
116	
117	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
118	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
119	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
120	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
121	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
122	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
123	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
124	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
125	
126	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
127	map of `kToSection`. -/
128	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
129	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
130	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
131	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
132	
133	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
134	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
135	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
136	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
137	  rfl
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
140	natural in `U`. -/
141	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
142	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
143	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
144	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
145	  rfl
146	
147	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
148	`algebraMap`. -/
149	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
150	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
151	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
152	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
153	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
154	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
155	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
156	
157	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf

161	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]

163	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
164	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
165	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
166	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
167	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
168	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
169	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
170	      map_add' := fun x y => by
171	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
172	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
173	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
174	        congr 1
175	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
176	          (C := C) f r }
177	  map_id U := by
178	    ext x
179	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,

180	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
181	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
182	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
183	    ext x
184	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
185	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
186	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
187	
188	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
189	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
190	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
191	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
192	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
193	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
194	  rfl
195	
196	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

430	/-- **The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
431	curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6, p. 144, Proposition 6.13(a)).
432	
433	The invertible sheaf cut out (in the dual / `𝒦_C`-subsheaf packaging of
434	Hartshorne `ℒ(D)`) by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on the complement,
435	where `f_P ∈ 𝔪_P ∖ 𝔪_P²` is any uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C,P}`. The
436	result is independent of the choice of uniformiser (two uniformisers
437	differ by a unit) and is an invertible `𝒪_C`-module of rank one.
438	
439	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
440	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf
441	carrier used by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so
442	that `H⁰` and `H¹` of `𝒪_C(P)` are accessible via
443	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 0/1`).
444	
445	iter-187 body (per analogist `ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md`
446	Decision 3 + Step 5 of the 5-step recipe): bundle the `carrierPresheaf`
447	+ `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` of the iter-186/187 Hartshorne
448	subsheaf-of-`K_C` direct construction. The two `IsLocallyNoetherian` /
449	`IsRegularInCodimensionOne` hypotheses propagate from the existing
450	`namespace lineBundleAtClosedPoint` variable block (already required by
451	every downstream consumer `globalSections_iff_*`,
452	`h1_vanishing_genusZero`, `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`,
453	`exists_nonconstant_genusZero`).
454	
455	Blueprint reference: `def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
456	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 144 + Proposition 6.13(a); Stacks tag 01X0). -/

462	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]

464	    (P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
465	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
466	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)

205	/-- **`kbar`-algebra structure on the function field `K(C)`**. The
206	project's `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraSection` instance gives
207	`Algebra kbar Γ(C.left, ⊤)`, and Mathlib's standard
208	`AlgebraicGeometry.instAlgebraCarrierObjOppositeOpens...` gives
209	`Algebra Γ(C.left, ⊤) K(C)` via `germToFunctionField`. Composing the
210	two algebra maps via `RingHom.toAlgebra` produces the desired
211	`Algebra kbar K(C)` instance, which is needed for `Submodule kbar K(C)`
212	to type-check. -/
213	noncomputable instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField
214	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
215	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsIntegral C.left] :
216	    Algebra kbar C.left.functionField := by
217	  haveI : Nonempty (⊤ : C.left.Opens) :=
218	    (AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff
219	      (X := C.left) (U := (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).mpr
220	      ⟨(inferInstance : Nonempty C.left).some, Set.mem_univ _⟩
221	  exact RingHom.toAlgebra
222	    ((Scheme.germToFunctionField C.left (⊤ : C.left.Opens)).hom.comp
223	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
224	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom)
225	
226	/-- **Carrier submodule** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U`: upgrade of
227	`carrierSet U` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the three closure proofs
228	(`0`, `+`, `kbar • _`). The closure proofs rest on the DVR-shipped
229	`Ring.ordFrac_add` (non-archimedean inequality on the discrete valuation
230	of a regular-in-codim-1 stalk; iter-186 Step 1 upgraded the carrier class
231	so this is invocable) and `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit` (the scalar from
232	`kbar` becomes a unit in the stalk, preserving the order under scalar
233	multiplication).
234	
235	iter-186 Step 2 (per analogist `ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md`
236	Decision 2): structural skeleton with the load-bearing Mathlib lemmas in
237	place; bookkeeping `sorry`s remain for the prover phase. -/
238	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule
239	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
240	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
241	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
242	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
243	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
244	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
245	    Submodule kbar C.left.functionField where
246	  carrier := lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh U
247	  zero_mem' := by
248	    -- `order Y 0 = WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0`;
249	    -- both `0 ≤ 0` and `-1 ≤ 0` hold trivially.
250	    refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
251	    · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
252	    · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
253	  add_mem' := by
254	    -- Case-split on `f + g = 0` (trivial via `WithZero.log_zero`), else
255	    -- apply `Ring.ordFrac_add` (DVR-shipped, Step 1 unlocks) and
256	    -- `WithZero.log` monotonicity on the nonzero part.
257	    rintro a b ⟨ha₁, ha₂⟩ ⟨hb₁, hb₂⟩
258	    have hMNZ : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (x : C.left.functionField),
259	        x ≠ 0 → Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) x ≠ 0 := by
260	      intro Q x hx; simp [hx]
261	    have key : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ),
262	        n ≤ 0 → n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q a →
263	          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q b →
264	          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
265	      intro Q n hn haₚ hbₚ
266	      by_cases hab : a + b = 0
267	      · simpa [hab, Scheme.RationalMap.order] using hn
268	      by_cases hae : a = 0
269	      · rw [hae, zero_add]; exact hbₚ
270	      by_cases hbe : b = 0
271	      · rw [hbe, add_zero]; exact haₚ
272	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
273	      have hoa : Ring.ordFrac R a ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q a hae
274	      have hob : Ring.ordFrac R b ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q b hbe
275	      have hoab : Ring.ordFrac R (a + b) ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q _ hab
276	      have hmin : Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b ≤ Ring.ordFrac R (a + b) :=
277	        Ring.ordFrac_add (R := R) a b hab
278	      have hlog : (Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b).log ≤
279	          Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
280	        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac R a) (Ring.ordFrac R b) with
281	            ⟨heq, _⟩ | ⟨heq, _⟩
282	        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hoa hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)
283	        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hob hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)
284	      have hminbd : n ≤ (Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b).log := by
285	        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac R a) (Ring.ordFrac R b) with
286	            ⟨heq, _⟩ | ⟨heq, _⟩
287	        · rw [heq]; exact haₚ
288	        · rw [heq]; exact hbₚ
289	      linarith
290	    refine ⟨fun Q hQU hQP => key Q 0 le_rfl (ha₁ Q hQU hQP) (hb₁ Q hQU hQP),
291	      fun hPU => key ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ (-1) (by norm_num) (ha₂ hPU) (hb₂ hPU)⟩
292	  smul_mem' := by
293	    -- For `c : kbar`, the scalar action `c • f` on `K(C)` factors through
294	    -- `algebraMap kbar K(C)`. For `c ≠ 0`, `algebraMap kbar K(C) c` lifts to
295	    -- a nonzero `β` in every stalk via the germ map; `Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero`
296	    -- on `β` then gives `order Q (algebraMap c) ≥ 0`, hence `c•x ∈ carrierSet`.
297	    intro c x ⟨hx₁, hx₂⟩
298	    rcases eq_or_ne c 0 with rfl | hc
299	    · -- `c = 0` ⇒ `0 • x = 0` ∈ carrierSet via `zero_mem'`.
300	      simp only [zero_smul]
301	      refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩ <;> simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
302	    rcases eq_or_ne x 0 with rfl | hx_ne
303	    · -- `x = 0` ⇒ `c • 0 = 0` ∈ carrierSet.
304	      simp only [smul_zero]
305	      refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩ <;> simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
306	    -- Both `c ≠ 0` and `x ≠ 0`: compute `order Q (c•x)` via multiplicativity.
307	    have hsmul : c • x = (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) * x :=
308	      Algebra.smul_def c x
309	    have hαne : (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) ≠ 0 := by
310	      have hinj := FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective kbar C.left.functionField
311	      simpa using hinj.ne_iff.mpr hc
312	    have hMNZ : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (y : C.left.functionField),
313	        y ≠ 0 → Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) y ≠ 0 := by
314	      intro Q y hy; simp [hy]
315	    have key_alpha_ge : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor),
316	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q
317	              (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) := by
318	      intro Q
319	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
320	      let β : R := (C.left.presheaf.germ (⊤ : C.left.Opens) Q.point trivial).hom
321	        ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
322	            (Opposite.op (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).hom c)
323	      have hα_eq : (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) =
324	          algebraMap R C.left.functionField β := by
325	        change ((Scheme.germToFunctionField C.left (⊤ : C.left.Opens)).hom
326	            ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
327	                (Opposite.op (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).hom c)) =
328	            (C.left.presheaf.stalkSpecializes
329	              ((genericPoint_spec C.left).specializes trivial)).hom β
330	        rw [← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes_apply
331	          (h := (genericPoint_spec C.left).specializes trivial)]
332	      have hβne : β ≠ 0 := by
333	        intro hzero; apply hαne; rw [hα_eq, hzero, map_zero]
334	      have hαord_ne : Ring.ordFrac R
335	          (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q _ hαne
336	      have hge : (1 : WithZero (Multiplicative ℤ)) ≤
337	          Ring.ordFrac R (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) := by
338	        rw [hα_eq]; exact Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero hβne
339	      unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
340	      rw [show (0 : ℤ) = WithZero.log (1 : WithZero (Multiplicative ℤ)) by simp]
341	      exact (WithZero.log_le_log (by norm_num) hαord_ne).mpr hge
342	    have key : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ),
343	        n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q x →
344	        n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (c • x) := by
345	      intro Q n hxn
346	      rw [hsmul]
347	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
348	      have hαnez := hMNZ Q _ hαne
349	      have hxnez := hMNZ Q x hx_ne
350	      unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
351	      rw [map_mul, WithZero.log_mul hαnez hxnez]
352	      have := key_alpha_ge Q
353	      unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order at this hxn
354	      linarith
355	    refine ⟨fun Q hQU hQP => key Q 0 (hx₁ Q hQU hQP),
356	      fun hPU => key ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ (-1) (hx₂ hPU)⟩
357	
358	/-- **The carrier presheaf of `𝒪_C(P)`** (iter-187 Step 3 of the
359	`carrierSet → carrierSubmodule → carrierPresheaf → isSheaf → Sheaf`
360	recipe from `analogies/ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md` Decision 3).
361	
362	Bundles `carrierSubmodule P hPcoh U` as the per-open value of a functor
363	`(Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat kbar`. The restriction map for
364	`f : U ⟶ V` in `(Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ` (i.e. `V.unop ⊆ U.unop` in opens)
365	is the `Submodule.inclusion` delivered by `carrierSet_mono`: as
366	`Submodule kbar K(C)`s, the carrier on `U` is included in the carrier
367	on `V`, and the inclusion is the identity-on-`K(C)` restriction. The
368	functor laws (`map_id`, `map_comp`) hold definitionally after
369	`Subtype`-extensionality. -/
370	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf
371	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
372	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
373	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
374	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
375	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
376	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} kbar where
377	  obj U := ModuleCat.of kbar
378	    ↥(lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule P hPcoh U)
379	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom <|
380	    Submodule.inclusion
381	      (p := lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule P hPcoh U)
382	      (p' := lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule P hPcoh V)
383	      (lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet_mono P hPcoh
384	        (CategoryTheory.leOfHom f.unop))
385	  map_id U := by ext ⟨x, _⟩; rfl
386	  map_comp _ _ := by ext ⟨x, _⟩; rfl
387	
388	/-- **Sheaf property of `carrierPresheaf`** (iter-187 Step 4 of the
389	`carrierSet → … → Sheaf` recipe). The Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C`
390	carrier inherits the sheaf condition from the underlying `Type`-valued
391	sheaf condition on its forget: each `carrierSubmodule U` is, as a set,
392	a subset of the function field `K(C)` (a constant presheaf on the
393	irreducible scheme `C.left`), and the per-prime-divisor order conditions
394	are stalk-local in the open.
395	
396	Per analogist `ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md` Decision 3
397	(`ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`), the route is `Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget`
398	applied to `CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`, mirroring the
399	project's `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` template
400	(`Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/SheafProperty.lean:32-37`).
401	
402	iter-187 Step 4 lands the *structural* lemma; the body of the
403	`Type`-valued sheaf condition (after the `isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget`
404	reduction) is a named typed `sorry` for the iter-187 prover round
405	(per the directive's "up to one narrow named typed sorry" allowance —
406	the gluing argument on the irreducible scheme `C.left` is ~30-50 LOC
407	of explicit `Presheaf.IsSheaf`-unfolding which Mathlib doesn't ship as
408	a one-liner for sub-presheaves of constants). -/
409	private lemma lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf_isSheaf
410	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
411	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
412	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
413	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
414	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
415	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
416	      (lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh) := by
417	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
418	        (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
419	  -- After the forget reduction, the goal is the `Type`-valued sheaf
420	  -- condition for `U ↦ ↥(carrierSubmodule P hPcoh U)`. Since each
421	  -- `carrierSubmodule U` is a subset of `C.left.functionField` and
422	  -- `C.left` is irreducible (from `IsIntegral C.left`), matching
423	  -- sections on a cover have a unique gluing in `K(C)`; the per-prime-
424	  -- divisor order conditions on `U` follow from the conditions on
425	  -- each cover element by stalk-locality. The full unfolding is
426	  -- ~30-50 LOC of explicit `Presheaf.IsSheaf`-arithmetic, deferred
427	  -- to the iter-187 prover round.
428	  sorry
429	
430	/-- **The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
431	curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6, p. 144, Proposition 6.13(a)).
432	
433	The invertible sheaf cut out (in the dual / `𝒦_C`-subsheaf packaging of
434	Hartshorne `ℒ(D)`) by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on the complement,
435	where `f_P ∈ 𝔪_P ∖ 𝔪_P²` is any uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C,P}`. The
436	result is independent of the choice of uniformiser (two uniformisers
437	differ by a unit) and is an invertible `𝒪_C`-module of rank one.
438	
439	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
440	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf
441	carrier used by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so
442	that `H⁰` and `H¹` of `𝒪_C(P)` are accessible via
443	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 0/1`).
444	
445	iter-187 body (per analogist `ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md`
446	Decision 3 + Step 5 of the 5-step recipe): bundle the `carrierPresheaf`
447	+ `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` of the iter-186/187 Hartshorne
448	subsheaf-of-`K_C` direct construction. The two `IsLocallyNoetherian` /
449	`IsRegularInCodimensionOne` hypotheses propagate from the existing
450	`namespace lineBundleAtClosedPoint` variable block (already required by
451	every downstream consumer `globalSections_iff_*`,
452	`h1_vanishing_genusZero`, `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`,
453	`exists_nonconstant_genusZero`).
454	
455	Blueprint reference: `def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
456	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 144 + Proposition 6.13(a); Stacks tag 01X0). -/
457	noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint
458	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
459	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
460	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
461	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
462	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
463	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
464	    (P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
465	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
466	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)

469	    lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf_isSheaf P hPcoh⟩

476	iter-183: `hPcoh` threaded through together with the sig amend on

481	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
482	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
483	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
484	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
485	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
486	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
487	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
488	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
489	    (s : Scheme.HModule kbar

12	discharges the sheaf condition for `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 7) and bundles the

14	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf is also packaged here. The downstream
15	finite-length carriers live in the sibling `Carriers.lean`.

31	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
32	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
33	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
34	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
35	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
36	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
37	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1

40	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
41	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
42	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
43	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
44	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩

46	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
47	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
48	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
49	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`

51	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/

55	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
56	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
57	  Iso.refl _

156	/-! ## §3. The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor -/
157	
158	/-- **The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve
159	`C / k̄`.**
160	
161	By definition,
162	`ℓ(D) := dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D)) ∈ ℕ`,
163	the `k̄`-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf
164	`𝒪_C(D)` associated to `D`. Finiteness of `ℓ(D)` is a consequence of the
165	coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper `k̄`-scheme
166	(Hartshorne II.5.19 / III.5.2), the same input that backs the finiteness
167	of `genus C`.
168	
169	The classical *complete linear system* `|D|` has `k̄`-projective dimension
170	`ℓ(D) − 1`; the chapter never uses this projective interpretation —
171	`ℓ(D)` is the only quantity consumed by the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain.
172	
173	Blueprint reference: `def:l_invariant` (Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295,
174	"We denote `dim_k H⁰(X, 𝒪(D))` by `l(D)`"). -/
175	noncomputable def l (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) : ℕ :=
176	  Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf (C := C) D) 0)
177	
178	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
179	
180	/-! ## §4. The χ-identity: `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`
181	
182	The bridge from the structural Euler characteristic to the arithmetic
183	degree of a divisor. Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3 reduces Riemann–Roch to
184	this identity; the proof is the inductive `D ↔ D + [P]` step (additivity
185	of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence, with base case `D = 0`
186	giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` from `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` and the definition of
187	the genus). The closure of the body is iter-175+ work after the
188	`Euler-characteristic additivity on a short exact sequence`-style
189	project-side helper is supplied and the `RR.3` sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` has a
190	body.
191	
192	**Iter-181 Lane H factoring.** Both Hartshorne IV.1.3 inputs (the base
193	case `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` and the inductive step `χ(𝒪_C(D + Y)) = χ(𝒪_C(D))
194	+ n`) intrinsically reference the body of `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf`,
195	which is still a typed-`sorry` placeholder waiting on the sibling
196	chapter `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`). We therefore factor the proof
197	into two named substantive helper lemmas (3-tier disclosure: **honest
198	named-sorry helpers** — each helper has a substantive type encoding a
199	nontrivial mathematical claim about the `sheafOf` line bundle whose
200	closure is downstream of the `RR.3` body):
201	
202	1. `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` — base case `χ(sheafOf 0) = 1 − g`,
203	   which on closure of `RR.3` reduces to `sheafOf 0 = toModuleKSheaf C`
204	   plus the Hartshorne I.3.4 input `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` plus the
205	   definition of the genus.
206	2. `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` — inductive step
207	   `χ(sheafOf (Finsupp.single Y n + D)) = χ(sheafOf D) + n` for any
208	   `Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor` and `n : ℤ`. On closure of `RR.3` this
209	   reduces to the Hartshorne IV.1.3 SES additivity argument iterated
210	   `|n|` times (with sign for `n < 0`).
211	
212	The main theorem then closes by induction on the `Finsupp`-structure of
213	`D : C.left.WeilDivisor` (via `Finsupp.induction`), using the two
214	helpers and the additivity of `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree`. -/
215

217	On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the global
218	sections of the structure sheaf form a one-dimensional `k̄`-vector space:
219	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1`.
220	
221	This is the Hartshorne~I.3.4 statement "for any projective variety `X`

229	**iter-183 Lane H status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. Body iter-184+

590	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`.
591	
592	Blueprint reference: `thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus`
593	(Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3, p. 295).
594	
595	**Iter-181 Lane H status** — the main theorem is closed by induction on
596	the `Finsupp` structure of `D`, consuming the two `sheafOf`-side helper
597	sorries `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` (base case) and
598	`eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` (inductive step). The body
599	itself is honest; its `sorryAx`-transitivity is the inevitable
600	consequence of `sheafOf` still being a typed sorry waiting on `RR.3`. -/
601	theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus
602	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
603	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
604	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
605	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
606	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
607	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D)
608	      = (Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D) + 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) := by
609	  -- Unfold `WeilDivisor` to expose the underlying `Finsupp` structure so
610	  -- `Finsupp.induction` applies directly.
611	  unfold Scheme.WeilDivisor at D
612	  induction D using Finsupp.induction with
613	  | zero =>
614	    -- D = 0: χ(sheafOf 0) = 1 - g by helper 1, and degree 0 = 0.
615	    exact (Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero C).trans
616	      (by simp [Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree])
617	  | single_add Y n D' _hY _hn hD' =>
618	    -- D = single Y n + D': use helper 2 then the inductive hypothesis.
619	    rw [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add, hD']
620	    -- Goal: (degree D' + 1 - g) + n = degree (single Y n + D') + 1 - g.
621	    have hdeg : Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree
622	        ((Finsupp.single Y n + D' : C.left.WeilDivisor))
623	        = n + Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D' := by
624	      change ((Finsupp.single Y n + D' : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)).sum
625	          (fun _ z => z) = _
626	      classical
627	      rw [Finsupp.sum_add_index (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ _ _ => rfl)]
628	      simp [Finsupp.sum_single_index]
629	      rfl
630	    linarith
631	
632	/-! ## §5. The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero -/
633	
634	/-- **Riemann–Roch in genus zero (Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5).**
635	
636	Let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over the
637	algebraically closed field `k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, and let `D ∈ Div(C)` with
638	`deg D ≥ 0`. Then
639	`ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1`.
640	
641	The proof specialises the χ-identity
642	`eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` to `g = 0`,
643	unfolds `χ` via `def:eulerChar_curve`, and absorbs the `H¹`-vanishing
644	hypothesis (named premise `_hH1`: `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0`, which is the
645	`H¹`-vanishing of a non-negative-degree invertible sheaf on a
646	genus-`0` curve, to be discharged by `RR.3` once `𝒪_C(D)` has a body and
647	the cohomology of `𝒪_{ℙ¹}(d)` is computed).
648	
649	Blueprint reference: `thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero` (Hartshorne IV.1
650	Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/
651	theorem Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero
652	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
653	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
654	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
655	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
656	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0)
657	    (_hdeg : (0 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D)
658	    (_hH1 : Module.finrank kbar
659	      (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) 1) = 0) :
660	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.l (C := C) D : ℤ)
661	      = Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D + 1 := by
662	  have h := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus C D
663	  simp only [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic, _hg, _hH1,
664	    Nat.cast_zero, sub_zero] at h
665	  exact h
666	
667	end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- The inclusion `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` of global sections of
`𝒪_C(P)` into the function field, viewing each section as a rational
function via the canonical embedding `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C` (Hartshorne II §6 p.
144).  The body lands together with the body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`.

iter-183: `hPcoh` threaded through together with the sig amend on
`lineBundleAtClosedPoint`, since the body of `toFunctionField` will
unfold the `carrierSet`-based body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` (the
`carrierSet` references `⟨P, hPcoh⟩` as a prime divisor). -/
noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
    (s : Scheme.HModule kbar
      (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0) :
    C.left.functionField :=
  sorry
input.new_string:
/-- The inclusion `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` of global sections of
`𝒪_C(P)` into the function field, viewing each section as a rational
function via the canonical embedding `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C` (Hartshorne II §6 p.
144).

iter-187 body (per directive Lane A cascade-close): now that
`lineBundleAtClosedPoint` has its substantive body
`⟨carrierPresheaf P hPcoh, carrierPresheaf_isSheaf P hPcoh⟩` (Step 5
of the refactor), a global section
`s : HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP hPcoh) 0` is converted
into an element of `K(C)` by:

1. Applying `Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv` to view `s` as a sheaf
   morphism `f : (constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) ⟶
   lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP hPcoh`.
2. Evaluating the underlying presheaf morphism at the top open `⊤`,
   giving a `kbar`-linear map from the constant-sheaf-fibre at `⊤`
   into the carrier submodule on `⊤`.
3. Feeding in the unit-image of `(1 : kbar)` under the
   `constantSheafAdj` adjunction unit (which sends `kbar` into the
   constant-sheaf-fibre at the terminal open `⊤`).
4. Extracting the underlying `K(C)`-value via the `Submodule.subtype`
   coercion `.1`.

iter-183: `hPcoh` threaded through together with the sig amend on
`lineBundleAtClosedPoint`, since the body of `toFunctionField` will
unfold the `carrierSet`-based body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` (the
`carrierSet` references `⟨P, hPcoh⟩` as a prime divisor). -/
noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
    (s : Scheme.HModule kbar
      (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0) :
    C.left.functionField := by
  -- Terminal-object witness for the constant-sheaf adjunction.
  set hT : Limits.IsTerminal (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :=
    Preorder.isTerminalTop _
  -- Step 1: view `s` as a sheaf morphism via the `Ext`-to-`Hom` bridge.
  let f := (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar
    (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh)) s
  -- Step 2: forget to a presheaf morphism.
  let g := (CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map f
  -- Step 3: unit-image of `(1 : kbar)` in the constant-sheaf-fibre at `⊤`.
  let one_image :=
    ((CategoryTheory.constantSheafAdj
        (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) hT).unit.app
      (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar)
  -- Step 4: evaluate `g` at `⊤`, apply to `one_image`, extract the underlying
  -- `K(C)`-value from the `carrierSubmodule P hPcoh (op ⊤)` carrier.

540	
541	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
542	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
543	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
544	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
545	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/
546	
547	/-- **Forward direction of `globalSections_iff` (Hartshorne II.7.7(b)).**
548	
549	Given `f ∈ K(C)^×` with `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P`
550	and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`, the rational function `f` lifts to a global section
551	`s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under the canonical inclusion
552	`𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f`. Hartshorne's construction inside the
553	proof of Proposition 7.7(b) (p. 157) defines this section locally on the
554	affine cover witnessing the Cartier-divisor structure of `[P]`: on a
555	neighbourhood `U_i` of `P` the section is `f · f_P` (in
556	`𝒪_C(P)(U_i) = f_P⁻¹ · 𝒪_C(U_i)`, this is `(f · f_P) · f_P⁻¹ = f`);
557	on the complement `C ∖ {P}` the section is `f` directly (the order
558	conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` guarantee `f ∈ 𝒪_C(U) = 𝒪_C(P)(U)`
559	on any affine open `U ⊆ C ∖ {P}`).
560	
561	**iter-181 PARTIAL — Mathlib-gap blocker** (per iter-181 Lane A
562	directive): the body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` (line ~140) is still
563	a typed `sorry` (gated on Sheaf-internal-Hom + ModuleCat-forget Mathlib
564	gaps analysed in iter-180 Lane D task_result), and the body of
565	`lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField` (line ~154) is similarly a
566	typed `sorry` (gated on the body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`). Until
567	those bodies land, the section `s` cannot be constructed concretely and
568	no proof of `toFunctionField P hP s = f` can be exhibited. This helper
569	is therefore an honest typed `sorry`, replacing the iter-180 Lane D
570	internal sub-sorry with a named declaration so iter-182+ provers can
571	attack each direction independently. -/

596	reading off the stalk-by-stalk DVR identification. Concretely:
597	
598	* At a prime divisor `Q ≠ P`, the stalk `𝒪_C(P)_Q = 𝒪_{C, Q}` agrees with
599	  the structure sheaf (since `𝒪_C(P)` equals `𝒪_C` on the open

602	* At `P`, the stalk `𝒪_C(P)_P = f_P⁻¹ · 𝒪_{C, P}` (where `f_P` is a
603	  uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C, P}`); the germ of `s` at `P` lies in

    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
    (_h : ∃ s : Scheme.HModule kbar
        (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0,
        lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
          (C := C) P hP hPcoh s = f) :

**iter-187 closure**: now that `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` and
`toFunctionField` both have substantive bodies (Steps 5 of the iter-187
refactor + the prover-phase cascade-close of `toFunctionField` body),
the order conditions on `f` are extracted directly from the
`carrierSubmodule` membership encoded in the underlying
`carrierSet`-element produced by `toFunctionField`'s linearEquiv chain.
Concretely: `toFunctionField s = ((sheafToPresheaf.map (linearEquiv₀ s)).
app(op ⊤) (one_image_of_1)).1`, an element of the function field whose
carrier-submodule membership witness `.2` says exactly that `f` (after
identification with `.1`) satisfies the order conditions on the top
open. Membership in `carrierSubmodule P hPcoh (op ⊤)` unfolds to the
desired pair of order conditions at all prime divisors (with the
`Q.point ∈ Set.univ` premise discharged by `Set.mem_univ _`). -/
private lemma globalSections_iff_mpr
    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
    (_h : ∃ s : Scheme.HModule kbar
        (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0,
        lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
          (C := C) P hP hPcoh s = f) :
    (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f := by
  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := _h
  set hT : Limits.IsTerminal (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :=
    Preorder.isTerminalTop _
  set sheaf_hom := (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar
    (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh)) s
  set g := (CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map sheaf_hom
  set one_image := ((CategoryTheory.constantSheafAdj
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) hT).unit.app
    (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar)
  set sec :=
    (g.app (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom one_image
  have hsec_mem : sec.1 ∈ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule P hPcoh
      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) := sec.2
  have hsec_eq : sec.1 = f := hs
  rw [hsec_eq] at hsec_mem
  exact ⟨fun Q hQP => hsec_mem.1 Q (Set.mem_univ _) hQP,
    hsec_mem.2 (Set.mem_univ _)⟩

  have hf_in : f ∈ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule P hPcoh

    refine ⟨fun Q _ hQP => _hord.1 Q hQP, fun _ => _hord.2⟩
  set F := lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh
  let φ : kbar →ₗ[kbar] ↑(lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule P hPcoh
      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
    LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ ⟨f, hf_in⟩
  let φ_cat : ModuleCat.of kbar kbar ⟶ F.val.obj
      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) :=
    ModuleCat.ofHom φ
  let adj := (CategoryTheory.constantSheafAdj
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) hT)
  let sheafHom := (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F).symm φ_cat
  let s : Scheme.HModule kbar F 0 :=
    (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F).symm sheafHom
  refine ⟨s, ?_⟩
  change ((((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map
    ((Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) s)).app
    (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom
      (((CategoryTheory.constantSheafAdj
        (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) hT).unit.app
        (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar))).1 = f
  have h1 : (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) s = sheafHom := by simp [s]
  rw [h1]
  have h3 := adj.homEquiv_unit (X := ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) (Y := F) (f := sheafHom)
  have h_simp : (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F) sheafHom = φ_cat :=
    (adj.homEquiv _ _).apply_symm_apply _
  rw [h_simp] at h3
  have h_eq := congr_arg (fun (k : ModuleCat.of kbar kbar ⟶ _) => k.hom (1 : kbar)) h3
  -- h_eq : φ_cat.hom 1 = (unit ≫ G.map sheafHom).hom 1
  simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
    CategoryTheory.sheafSections_obj_map] at h_eq
  -- Now h_eq : φ_cat.hom 1 = (sheafHom.val.app (op T)).hom (unit.hom 1)
  -- Goal expects ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).map sheafHom).app (op T), not sheafHom.val.app (op T)
  rw [CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf_map]
  -- Now goal matches the RHS of h_eq
  rw [← h_eq]
  -- Goal: φ_cat.hom 1 .1 = f

  simp [φ_cat, φ, LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]

**iter-181 PARTIAL — Mathlib-gap blocker** (per iter-181 Lane A
directive): the body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` (line ~140) is still
a typed `sorry` (gated on Sheaf-internal-Hom + ModuleCat-forget Mathlib
gaps analysed in iter-180 Lane D task_result), and the body of
`lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField` (line ~154) is similarly a
typed `sorry` (gated on the body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`). Until
those bodies land, the section `s` cannot be constructed concretely and
no proof of `toFunctionField P hP s = f` can be exhibited. This helper
is therefore an honest typed `sorry`, replacing the iter-180 Lane D
internal sub-sorry with a named declaration so iter-182+ provers can
attack each direction independently. -/
private lemma globalSections_iff_mp
    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
    (_hord : (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f) :
    ∃ s : Scheme.HModule kbar
        (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0,
      lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
        (C := C) P hP hPcoh s = f := by
  -- Mathlib gap: `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` body + `toFunctionField` body
  -- are both typed `sorry` (lines 140, 154). The forward construction of
  -- Hartshorne II.7.7(b) requires unfolding the Cartier-divisor sheaf
  -- structure of `𝒪_C(P)`, which is unavailable until the upstream bodies
  -- land. iter-181 Lane A PARTIAL: directional helper sorry.
  sorry

`constantSheafAdj.homEquiv.symm` into a sheaf morphism, then through

round-trip `toFunctionField s = f` follows from the unit/counit
equation `adj.homEquiv_unit` evaluated at the input `(1 : kbar)`. -/
private lemma globalSections_iff_mp
    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
    (_hord : (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f) :
    ∃ s : Scheme.HModule kbar
        (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0,
      lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
        (C := C) P hP hPcoh s = f := by
  set hT : Limits.IsTerminal (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :=
    Preorder.isTerminalTop _
  have hf_in : f ∈ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule P hPcoh

    refine ⟨fun Q _ hQP => _hord.1 Q hQP, fun _ => _hord.2⟩
  set F := lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh

      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
    LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ ⟨f, hf_in⟩
  let φ_cat : ModuleCat.of kbar kbar ⟶ F.val.obj
      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) :=
    ModuleCat.ofHom φ
  set adj := (CategoryTheory.constantSheafAdj
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) hT)
  let sheafHom := (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F).symm φ_cat
  let s : Scheme.HModule kbar F 0 :=
    (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F).symm sheafHom
  refine ⟨s, ?_⟩
  change ((((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map
    ((Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) s)).app
    (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom
      ((adj.unit.app (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar))).1 = f
  have h1 : (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) s = sheafHom := by simp [s]
  rw [h1]
  have h3 := adj.homEquiv_unit (X := ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) (Y := F) (f := sheafHom)
  have h_simp : (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F) sheafHom = φ_cat :=
    (adj.homEquiv _ _).apply_symm_apply _

  have hrew : ((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map sheafHom).app
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) =
      ((CategoryTheory.sheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj
          (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).map sheafHom :=
    rfl
  rw [hrew]
  -- Use `h3` and `ModuleCat.comp_apply` to identify the goal's chain with `φ_cat.hom 1`.
  rw [show ((((CategoryTheory.sheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).map sheafHom)).hom
        ((adj.unit.app (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar)) = φ_cat.hom 1 from by
    rw [h3]; exact (ModuleCat.comp_apply _ _ _).symm]
  -- Now the goal is `(φ_cat.hom 1).1 = f`. `φ_cat.hom = φ`, and `φ 1 = ⟨f, hf_in⟩`.
  show (φ_cat.hom 1).1 = f
  show (φ 1).1 = f

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean

## Summary

**Entering iter-187**: 7 sorries (post refactor-Steps 3+4+5 land,
which closed L397 `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` body but introduced the
new L409 `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` sorry — net 0).

**Exit iter-187**: **4 sorries** (net **−3** from prover phase). HARD
BAR met (Step 5 of refactor closed pre-existing L397; prover phase
closed 3 additional cascade sorries).

### Sorries closed (3)

1. `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField` body (L480) — was sorry,
   now substantive linearEquiv chain.
2. `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff_mp` (L572) — was sorry,
   now full proof via adjunction round-trip.
3. `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff_mpr` (L617) — was sorry,
   now full proof via `carrierSubmodule` membership extraction.

### Sorries remaining (4)

1. `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` (L409) — sheaf-condition body
   (refactor-introduced narrow named sorry per Step 4 allowance).
2. `h1_vanishing_genusZero` (L788) — gated on LES + skyscraper
   cohomology machinery.
3. `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` (L825) — gated on `h1_vanishing` + a
   χ-identity adapted from `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` to
   `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`.
4. `exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (L879) — gated on `dim_eq_two`.

## lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField (L480)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Substantive linearEquiv₀ chain. Given
  `s : HModule kbar F 0`, apply `Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv` to
  get a sheaf morphism `f : (constantSheaf _).obj kbar ⟶ F`; forget
  via `sheafToPresheaf.map`; evaluate at `op ⊤` (top open is terminal
  in `Opens C.left.toTopCat` via `Preorder.isTerminalTop`); apply to
  the unit-image of `(1 : kbar)` under `constantSheafAdj.unit.app`;
  extract `.1` to get the `K(C)`-value from the
  `carrierSubmodule P hPcoh (op ⊤)` carrier.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — body lands axiom-clean.
- **Key insight:** `Preorder.isTerminalTop` provides the
  `IsTerminal ⊤` witness needed by `constantSheafAdj`. The
  sheaf-fibre-at-`⊤` content of a `(constantSheaf _).obj kbar`
  morphism is extracted via the standard adjunction unit.

## lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff_mpr (L617)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Given `⟨s, hs⟩` with `hs : toFunctionField P hP hPcoh s = f`,
  the underlying element of `toFunctionField`'s body is
  `((sheafHom.val.app (op ⊤)).hom (one_image)).val` (where `sheafHom =
  HModule_zero_linearEquiv s`). Its `.2` witness gives membership in
  `carrierSubmodule P hPcoh (op ⊤)`. Combined with `hs` (which says
  `.1 = f`), we deduce `f ∈ carrierSubmodule(⊤)`, which unfolds to the
  desired order conditions (with `Q.point ∈ Set.univ` discharged via
  `Set.mem_univ _`).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — body lands axiom-clean.
- **Key insight:** Membership in `carrierSubmodule P hPcoh (op ⊤)`
  *is* (up to trivial `Set.univ` discharge) the order conditions
  required by the `iff`. The proof is a one-shot extraction.

## lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff_mp (L572)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** The order conditions `_hord` express exactly
  `f ∈ carrierSubmodule P hPcoh (op ⊤)`. From `⟨f, hf_in⟩`, build a
  `kbar`-linear map `φ : kbar → ↥(carrierSubmodule(⊤))` via
  `LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ ⟨f, hf_in⟩` (sending `1 ↦ ⟨f, hf_in⟩`);
  lift to `φ_cat := ModuleCat.ofHom φ`; transport via
  `constantSheafAdj.homEquiv.symm` into a sheaf morphism `sheafHom`;
  transport via `HModule_zero_linearEquiv.symm` into the `HModule`
  element `s`. Round-trip identity follows from
  `adj.homEquiv_unit sheafHom = unit.app X ≫ G.map sheafHom = φ_cat`,
  applied at `(1 : kbar)` via `ModuleCat.comp_apply`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — body lands axiom-clean.
- **Tactical detail:** Direct `rw [← h_eq]` failed to match the goal
  pattern (despite visual identity — probably a subtle
  `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` vs `.hom` elaboration difference under
  coercion). Workaround: lift the equation through the `↑` coercion
  with `congr_arg Subtype.val h_eq` and chain via `.trans`.
- **Key insight:** `sheafSections.obj.map sheafHom = sheafToPresheaf.map
  sheafHom .app (op ⊤)` holds by `rfl` since `sheafSections :=
  sheafToPresheaf.flip`. This bridges the goal's `sheafToPresheaf.map`
  form to `adj.homEquiv_unit`'s `G.map` form.

## carrierPresheaf_isSheaf (L409)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** `Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget` was already
  applied by the refactor. The remaining goal is the `Type`-valued
  sheaf condition for `U ↦ ↥(carrierSubmodule U)` (a sub-presheaf of
  the constant `K(C)` sheaf on an irreducible scheme).
- **Result:** FAILED — `aesop` makes no progress. The substantive
  closure requires explicit gluing-on-stalk-conditions ~30-50 LOC of
  `Presheaf.IsSheaf`-unfolding which Mathlib doesn't ship as a one-liner.
- **Next step iter-188+:** explicit gluing argument: for matching
  sections `(s_i ∈ carrierSubmodule(U_i))` on a cover `(U_i → U)`,
  pick any `s_i.val ∈ K(C)`, verify it satisfies the order conditions
  on `U` by reading off from each `U_i` (since `(U_i)` cover `U`).

## h1_vanishing_genusZero (L788)

### Attempt 0 (deferred — Mathlib substrate gap)
- **Approach (intended):** assemble the closed-point short exact
  sequence `0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`, feed to LES of
  `Scheme.HModule kbar`-cohomology (forget-functor naturality from
  `Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence_exact`), substitute
  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0` (genus-0 unfolding) and
  `H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper, Hartshorne III.2.5).
- **Result:** NOT ATTEMPTED THIS ITER — requires substantial
  cohomology-LES + skyscraper-vanishing infrastructure not currently in
  the project. Gating on iter-188+ infrastructure work.

## dim_eq_two_of_genusZero (L825)

### Attempt 0 (deferred — adapter to RR.2 missing)
- **Approach (intended):** invoke
  `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` (from
  `RR.2 RRFormula.lean`) on `D = ofClosedPoint P hP`. This requires
  an identification `lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP hPcoh ≅
  Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (ofClosedPoint P hP)` (both should be the
  same invertible sheaf, but the project's two constructions don't yet
  carry a shared API to identify them).
- **Result:** NOT ATTEMPTED THIS ITER — gated on the
  `lineBundleAtClosedPoint ≅ WeilDivisor.sheafOf` adapter. Iter-188+
  follow-up.

## exists_nonconstant_genusZero (L879)
